notes.introduction: An exposition of Colossians 2:1-3 on Paul's expressed concern for believers in Colossae, Laodicea, and beyond, set within the broader context of false teaching threatening early churches. The passage is examined under three headings: the conflict Paul reveals, the reasons behind it, and the means by which it may be addressed.

sections.heading: Review of Colossians 1 and Ministry Philosophy
The Nature and Scope of the Conflict
The Reason for the Conflict: False Teaching and Enemy Activity
The Church at Laodicea: A Contrasting Threat
Resolving the Conflict Through Spiritual Growth
Comfort, Unity, and Maturity in Colossians 2:2-3
Practical Warning Against False Teachers Infiltrating the Church
